tasks.title: Create user documentation
tasks.summary: Give a new user enough context to install, configure, run, and extend agent-tracker without reading the source first. Include the dogfooding workflow in the user-facing docs.
validation_checks: Manual docs review: a new user can create a minimal project config and task plan from docs alone.
Manual dogfood review: an agent can pull the next available task using tracking/README.md.
tasks.next_action: Expand README and docs so installation, config, task plans, CLI lifecycle, plugin authoring, spool ingestion, logging, and self-dogfooding are documented.

tasks.title: Add config validation and schema versioning
tasks.summary: Fail early with actionable errors for malformed project configs and establish a compatibility path for future config and database changes.
tasks.next_action: Add config_schema_version support, validate config fields, record database schema metadata, and cover malformed config plus schema metadata in tests.

requirements.description: Documentation should define the current user-facing config and task-plan contracts before versioning them.

tasks.title: Implement pull-based spool support
tasks.summary: Support a safe cross-directory spool bridge before adding network transports.
tasks.next_action: Add pull-spool with dry-run, local/shared-filesystem copy, partial-file skipping, and idempotent behavior before ingest-spool.

requirements.description: Spool config shape should be validated before adding remote/local path semantics.

tasks.title: Add an MCP server entrypoint
tasks.summary: Package the existing MCP-friendly handlers as a real MCP server adapter while keeping business logic in the service layer.
tasks.next_action: Add a thin server adapter around AgentTrackerTools and test tool registration/payloads without duplicating Coordinator logic.

requirements.description: Keep local-first spool and config contracts stable before packaging another adapter.

tasks.title: Add approval gates and authority metadata
tasks.summary: Prevent automatic claims or completions for tasks that need human approval or stronger authority checks.
tasks.next_action: Define authority metadata, approval transitions, claim filtering, and audit entries for approval and denial decisions.

requirements.description: Adapter identity and tool payloads should be clear before enforcing authority rules.

tasks.title: Move Markdown note and result export into plugins
tasks.summary: Keep SQLite as live state while making git-backed summaries derived exports instead of manually edited coordination state.

execution.adapter_note: Project-specific HPC output belongs outside core.

tasks.next_action: Define exporter expectations for readable Markdown summaries and keep large artifacts linked rather than copied.

requirements.description: Approval and audit semantics should be visible before adding richer derived exports.

tasks.title: Add deterministic follow-up task proposals
tasks.summary: Let project plugins propose follow-up tasks from completed work or events without giving callbacks direct authority to launch agents.
tasks.next_action: Wire FollowupPlanner into service and CLI, persist proposed tasks separately from imported tasks, and support human review before promotion.

requirements.description: Readable exports should be available before adding proposal review state.

tasks.title: Add a cheap attendant command
tasks.summary: Provide a scheduled command that handles routine coordination without keeping high-intelligence agents running as pollers.
tasks.next_action: Add an idempotent command that ingests spool files, recovers stale leases, exports snapshots, proposes follow-ups, and reports concise counts.

requirements.description: Attendant should call stable follow-up proposal APIs rather than inventing task creation behavior.

tasks.title: Vendor a generic project-manager skill
tasks.summary: Provide a reusable project-manager Codex skill without an hpc-ci-specific name, and make it installable from this package in new environments.

execution.notes: The vendored skill should be generic. HPC-specific workflows belong in project notebooks or repo-local extensions.

validation_checks: Manual check: the skill is named project-manager, not hpc-ci-project-manager.
Manual check: a new install can copy the vendored skill into a Codex skills directory.
tasks.next_action: Finish documenting and validating the vendored project-manager skill bootstrap flow, including how hpc-ci-project-tracker should consume or extend it.

requirements.description: User docs should establish the expected skill and dogfooding workflows before expanding installation guidance.

tasks.title: Add repo and project notebook conventions
tasks.summary: Define where project and repo notebooks live, what they contain, how prompt renderers should use them, and where to move currently stashed planning context.
validation_checks: Manual check: stashed planning context has a durable home or an explicit migration path.
tasks.next_action: Design notebook artifact conventions and decide where the coordination intake/dispatch research note should live long term.

requirements.description: The base documentation should exist before adding notebook conventions to it.

tasks.title: Add an intake inbox for ideas, features, and checks
tasks.summary: Capture rough user ideas, feature requests, checks, and planning notes as raw non-claimable intake for later project-manager triage.
validation_checks: Manual check: intake records are not returned as claimable tasks.
tasks.next_action: Add raw intake records and CLI/service flows for capturing ideas/features/checks without promoting them into active tasks.

requirements.description: The proposal model should exist before adding a broader human intake path.

tasks.title: Add project-manager intake triage workflow
tasks.summary: Let a project-manager agent organize intake, ask planning questions, and promote selected items into proposed task contracts.
validation_checks: Manual check: project-manager triage creates proposed tasks, not immediately claimable tasks.
tasks.next_action: Add deterministic project-manager triage commands and skill guidance for promoting intake into proposed tasks.

requirements.description: Raw intake must exist before a triage workflow can organize it.
The generic project-manager skill should exist before triage guidance depends on it.

tasks.title: Add a human intervention queue
tasks.summary: Model human intervention needs as durable queue state before sending external notifications.
validation_checks: Manual check: intervention reasons are small, explicit, and require resolution evidence.
tasks.next_action: Define intervention reasons, state transitions, audit records, and resolution evidence before adding PR notification.

requirements.description: Approval and authority concepts should exist before broader intervention states.

tasks.title: Check repository setup for PR-based notifications
tasks.summary: Add checks that determine whether associated repositories can support PR-based human notification, with fallbacks for sandbox auth limitations.

execution.notes: Do not assume gh auth works inside agent sandboxes. Prefer explicit diagnostics and connector-compatible designs.

validation_checks: Manual check: setup diagnostics distinguish missing remote, missing PR, missing auth, and unsupported sandbox.
tasks.next_action: Design and implement PR notification setup diagnostics before any notifier tries to post comments.

requirements.description: Notification setup should target durable intervention states.

tasks.title: Add idempotent PR notification exporter
tasks.summary: Notify the user about intervention states through PR comments or prepared PR notification payloads while keeping SQLite as canonical state.
validation_checks: Manual check: repeated exports update or suppress duplicate notifications instead of spamming PR comments.
tasks.next_action: Add an idempotent PR notification exporter with a dry-run/prepared-payload fallback for sandboxes without GitHub auth.

requirements.description: Setup diagnostics should exist before notification posting is attempted.

tasks.title: Define HPC validation request contract
tasks.summary: Define how feature agents request HPC validation without owning long-running Slurm lifecycle work.

execution.notes: Keep validation_request, callback_event, suite_verdict, and codex_review as separate records.

validation_checks: Manual check: the contract does not let callbacks launch high-value agents directly.
tasks.next_action: Document and model validation requests separately from callback events, suite verdicts, and Codex review reports.

requirements.description: The intake/proposal path should exist before adding specialized validation request records.

tasks.title: Add Codex SDK/App Server worker adapter
tasks.summary: Allow queued tasks to be submitted to Codex programmatically without manually creating GUI chats.

execution.notes: Treat Codex App Server/SDK as an execution adapter. SQLite remains canonical for task state, leases, approvals, events, and audit records.

validation_checks: Manual check: worker records thread/prompt/report/evidence metadata without exposing local app-server control over unsafe network boundaries.
tasks.next_action: Prototype a one-shot worker that claims a task, renders a prompt, invokes a Codex SDK/App Server thread, and records thread/report evidence.

requirements.description: Cheap attendant behavior should exist before adding high-value agent execution.
Adapter boundaries should be clear before adding another execution surface.
